body: Purpose: Defines the Shared Workflow shared App Behavior home and its ownership boundary.
Audience: Agents documenting shared frontend App Behavior and route packets that reference it.
Keep In Sync With: App Behavior shared specs README, full coverage taxonomy, and route/shared-surface inventory templates.
Update When: The shared ownership boundary, route reference rule, or backend authority boundary changes.
Does Not Cover: Route-local behavior, backend internals, or runtime implementation details.

content.title: Shared Workflow
content.body: This shared home governs cross-route flows, stored intent, return paths, list/detail patching, refresh-on-return, multi-step workflows, handoffs, and shared workflow proof.

content.title: Ownership Boundary
content.body: Put behavior here when it has shared frontend meaning, shared runtime source, shared proof, or repeated route usage. The shared spec should define the common frontend contract and the required proof expectations once.

content.title: Must Stay Route-Local
content.body: Keep route-specific entry/exit, route-local step copy, route-specific command inputs, route-specific success/error projection, and route proof of participation in the workflow in the route packet. The route packet may reference this shared spec but should not duplicate shared authority.

content.title: Backend Authority Only
content.body: Do not document backend workflow execution, server-side job orchestration, queues, and backend-only business process mechanics as frontend App Behavior authority. Reference backend/API authority when the frontend behavior depends on those facts.

content.title: Route Reference Shape
content.body: Route packets should reference this shared home and record the route-specific trigger, input, projection, override, proof row, or accepted gap. If a route differs from the shared behavior, document the route-specific override explicitly and route any product decision that cannot be derived.
